description: The 2024/25 Partnership Program (Identified Organisations) enables the Office of Sport to partner with key industry stakeholders to address priority issues and capitalise on opportunities to further areas of the Office of Sport Strategic Plan.

description: The 2025/26 Partnership Program (Identified Organisations) enables the Office of Sport to partner with key industry stakeholders to address priority issues and capitalise on opportunities to further areas of the Office of Sport Strategic Plan.

title: Pathways Development Program 2025/26
description: The Pathways Development Program is a competitive Screen NSW initiative that supports emerging writers with disability to develop original long-form screen projects to a pitch-ready stage.

title: Permit/Plug/Play Pilot Program
description: The Permit Plug Play Pilot Program is a partnership grant program with Pilot councils to help streamline approval processes for temporary road-closure enabled events and deliver enabling place infrastructure.

description: This program provides grants to eligible applicants for projects that provide protection of critical timber supplies in the Murray region.

title: Plumbing Industry Climate Action Centre Grant - Glenwood
description: This project aims to establish a vocational training centre in Glenwood (near Norwest in the City of Blacktown) referred to as a Hydrogen Centre of Excellence (HcoE), with the Plumbing Industry Climate Action Centre (PICAC).

title: Post Men's Behaviour Change Program (MBCP) Pilot Grant
description: The grant will provide funding for a pilot program to provide structured, ongoing support for men who have completed an MBCP, consistent with the NSW Practice Standards for Men’s Domestic Violence Behaviour Change Programs.

title: Postvention support for children, young people and families bereaved by suicide
description: The program will support children, young people and families to develop mental tools and coping skills to work through the feelings of guilt, shame and blame that are associated with suicide bereavement grief.

title: Prawn Fisher’s Financial Guidance and Training Support Program
description: The Financial Guidance and Training Support program will help fishing businesses transition to the long-term management of white spot virus with financial guidance and training funding to assist with decision making.

title: Premier’s Department Contingency Fund
description: The Premier’s Department Contingency Fund supports unanticipated Government commitments for one-off events, programs and initiatives occurring and announced by the Government outside of the Budget process, and which have no ongoing budget impact.

title: Preschools on non-government school sites program
description: This grant program provides funding to build new or upgrade existing preschools on at least 50 non-government school sites, at both independent and Catholic schools in NSW.

title: Project Support Program (PSP) 2023/24
description: The 2023/24 Project Support Program (‘the Program’) has been created to administer projects that have been identified by the NSW Government and that fall within the portfolio of the Minister for Sport.

description: The Protecting Our Communities (Disaster Resilience) Program has been established by the Australian Government to improve disaster resilience and preparedness in communities at a local level. These were Federal Election 2022 Commitments for NSW.

title: RRRF2025_Round 1
description: This initiative will provide State funding to assist local councils across regional NSW complete priority projects.

description: This project aims to help people increase their capacity to protect and conserve their local environment by delivering community support and education programs.

description: The WWHC Grant will strengthen, formalise and scale its existing work with men and boys through its Men’s Consultation Group (MCG).

description: These Ministerially Approved Grants (MAG) support non-government organisations to deliver specific health services and projects in Western Sydney Local Health District.

title: Invest and Trade WA

title: METS Innovation Program
amount_text: The METS Innovation Program seeks to broaden support for research projects led by METS companies servicing WA’s mining industry. The program supports the development of new technologies in the following strategic areas: low-emissions technologies, precision and low-impact mining, critical minerals, and the alternative use of tailings and waste.

title: Regional Development Commissions
amount_text: There are Regional Development Commissions for the Gascoyne, Great Southern, Goldfields-Esperance, Kimberley, Mid West, Peel, Pilbara, Southwest, and Wheatbelt regions. title: Find capital investment
description: Learn more about different ways to find money to fund your business. Attracting investment is a vital part of moving from vision to execution. Developing prototypes, testing and taking a product to market can require a lot of capital investment. There are many different ways for your business to attract funding for growth. You can look for investment privately or apply for loans or grant programs run by government.

title: Funding and support for social enterprises
description: Find out how to apply for the Social Enterprise Grant. This grant gives matched funding to help ACT social enterprises to start and grow. This grant program provides matched funding to help ACT social enterprises to start and grow. The Mill House Ventures, a social enterprise advisory, delivers the grant program for the ACT Government.

The Major Event Fund supports this commitment by delivering a mechanism for investing in, supporting and leveraging major events in Canberra. It has been established to support major event opportunities that can:

description: Find all current recipients on our website. Applications for Up-to-$5K funding are open for the 2025-26 financial year. Eligible Canberrans can also get help to pay rates, transport, health and education costs. ​ Visit the assistance website to find out more.

title: Aboriginal Business Development Program
description: Applications are closed and are not open for 2025. The Aboriginal Business Development program helps to support the start-up and development of Northern Territory (NT) Aboriginal businesses, especially those that address community needs through: Aboriginal business enterprises may be eligible for funding up to $30,000 (ex GST) - once every 3 years.

description: The Aboriginal Ranger Grant Program ended 30 June 2025. title: Aboriginal Tourism Grant Program Round 9 - closed
amount_text: Aboriginal Tourism Grant Program Round 9 - closed | Tourism and Events Northern Territory Skip to main content Search websiteCloseSearch MenuMain navigation About us Who we are Our vision Our corporate structure Partners Annual Report Work with us Job opportunities Contact usAbout usTourism and Events NT’s role is to increase the desirability of the Northern Territory as a destination, inspiring more people to visit, stay longer and spend more.Read more Strategy Research & Sector Research Latest
description: Tourism and Events NT’s role is to increase the desirability of the Northern Territory as a destination, inspiring more people to visit, stay longer and spend more. Strategic insights gleaned from research underpin Tourism and Events NT domestic and international activities. We promote the Northern Territory as unique holiday destination through cooperative marketing activity with wholesalers, online travel agents and airlines to capitalise on the high domestic awareness of the NT, packaging NT itineraries and experiences and creating a sense of urgency by encouraging consumers to ‘book now’ with enticing deals.

title: NT Health Aboriginal Scholarship Scheme
description: If you’re an Aboriginal student in your first year of an undergraduate health degree, you can apply for the Northern Territory (NT) Health Aboriginal Scholarship Scheme. Five scholarships worth up to $5,000 each are available to eligible students who are studying: Applications open at 8am on Monday 5 February 2024.

title: Arts Projects grant
description: Through the Arts Projects Grant, you can apply for funding to: Individuals, groups and organisations can apply for: Applications are assessed by a panel of peers.

title: Small Arts Projects Grant
description: Through the Small Arts Projects Grant, you can get funding for arts and cultural activities across the NT that increase community access, engagement and participation in the arts. Individuals, groups and organisations can apply for up to $5,000 each financial year. This grant is open until 31 May 2026, or until the funding runs out.

title: NXT Gen ARTS grants
description: Through the NXT Gen ARTS Grants, you can get funding to do a work placement with an NT arts organisation. This grant gives young people practical experience in the creative industries and helps them find jobs in the NT arts and culture sector. Individuals aged 18 to 25 can apply for up to $13,000 per financial year - $10,000 for the early career artist and $3,000 for the host organisation.

title: Professional Development Grant
description: Through the Professional Development Grant, you can get funding to support career development opportunities that build skills, knowledge, networks and leadership capacity, strengthening professional practice and raising the profile of NT artists and arts workers. Individuals can apply for up to $2,000 per financial year. If you live in a very remote part of Australia and your activity includes travel, you may be eligible for up to an additional $500 per financial year to help cover travel costs.

title: Quick Response Grant
description: Through the Quick Response Grant, you can get funding to access arts and cultural opportunities that arise at short notice. Individuals can apply for up to $1,000 per financial year. If you live in a very remote part of Australia and your activity includes travel, you may be eligible for up to an additional $500 per financial year to help cover travel costs.

title: Aboriginal Tourism Development in NT Parks and Reserves Grant Program

title: Agricultural Value-Add Grant - Round 2
description: The Agricultural Value-Add Grant supports Territory primary producers and agricultural-linked businesses to invest in projects that strengthen productivity, diversification, and value-adding across the NT agriculture sector. Round 2 of the Agricultural Value-Add Grant Program closed on 3 November 2025. Following a competitive assessment process, 8 Territory businesses have been awarded a total of $258,000 in funding.

title: Asset Assistance Grant
description: Non-profit organisations (NPO) whose assets have been significantly damaged as a direct result of an eligible disaster can apply to access the grant. You can apply for a grant of up to $10,000 to repair or replace an asset that: NPOs impacted by the Central Australia and Barkly Region Severe Weather and Floods, February 2026, in the local government area (LGA) of:

title: Biz Secure Program
description: The Biz Secure Program provides financial assistance to help Territory businesses improve the safety and security of their facilities against break-ins and crime. Security work for Biz Secure must be done by a registered supplier. Eligible businesses can apply for multiple security upgrades per premises, with a funding cap of $15,000 (GST exclusive) per location.

title: Book Now for Aboriginal Tourism Businesses Grant Program 2022
description: Tourism and Events NT’s role is to increase the desirability of the Northern Territory as a destination, inspiring more people to visit, stay longer and spend more. We promote the Northern Territory as unique holiday destination through cooperative marketing activity with wholesalers, online travel agents and airlines to capitalise on the high domestic awareness of the NT, packaging NT itineraries and experiences and creating a sense of urgency by encouraging consumers to ‘book now’ with enticing deals.

title: Business Growth Program
description: If you're a small business in the Northern Territory (NT), you may be able to get funding for professional advice, services and systems. Funding of $2,000 to $10,000 is available to reimburse 50% of the costs of eligible services provided by an approved service provider. You can access funding across a single or multiple applications within each financial year to a total of $10,000.

title: Business Innovation Program
description: This program is currently closed and under review. If you’re a Northern Territory (NT) start-up or entrepreneur with an innovative idea, you can apply for the Business Innovation Program (BIP). The program helps businesses develop and commercialise innovative concepts.

title: Cyber Invest Business Program
description: The Cyber Invest Business Program supports small to medium Territory businesses to uplift their cybersecurity and resilience. A cyberattack can be devasting for a business. Cyber Invest provides a grant up to $10,000 for small to medium Territory business to uplift their cybersecurity and resilience. If you're a Territory small to medium business find out how to apply for a Cyber Invest grant to reduce your cyber risk.

title: Electric Vehicle Charger (Residential and Business) Grants Scheme
description: Approved grants have until 31 May 2026 to be paid and finalised. If you own an electric vehicle (EV), you can apply for funding to buy and install a charger at your residential property or business. Through the EV Charger Grants Scheme, you can get grants of up to:

title: Energy Bill Relief - small business
description: Eligible small businesses can get up to $150 in energy bill relief from 1 July 2025 to 31 December 2025. If you're a household, read more about energy bill relief for households. You can get energy bill relief if your small business:

title: Freight Subsidy Disaster Assistance
description: If you’re a primary producer based in a disaster-affected Local Government Area (LGA), you may be eligible for freight subsidy disaster assistance. This assistance is jointly funded by the Commonwealth and Northern Territory governments under the Disaster Recovery Funding Arrangements. Financial assistance can be used for transport freight costs associated with:

title: Global Trade Scheme
description: The Global Trade Scheme helps eligible Northern Territory (NT) businesses set up or expand their exporting capabilities. The grant program includes 3 streams of support for businesses that: The program offers different levels of support depending on where the business currently is on its export journey. Each stream is based on a 50:50 co-contribution basis.

title: Grants for international student wellbeing
description: You can apply for a grant of up to $10,000 for events and activities that enhance the wellbeing of international students in the Northern Territory (NT). The international student wellbeing grant program provides funding to: Events and activities must take place from 1 July 2024 until 1 June 2025.

title: Home and Business Battery Scheme
description: The Home and Business Battery Scheme (HBBS) has now reached its $6 million funding allocation and is now closed to new grants. Applications that have been submitted but not yet approved, along with new applications received, will only be considered if funding is available. There is no guarantee that any application will be assessed or approved. While applicants may still choose to proceed with battery installation at their own expense, only installations approved through a formal HBBS grant notification will be eligible for funding. Where works are undertaken without prior HBBS approval, applicants will be responsible for the full costs.

title: Roadhouse to Recovery Grant
description: Tourism and Events NT’s role is to increase the desirability of the Northern Territory as a destination, inspiring more people to visit, stay longer and spend more. Strategic insights gleaned from research underpin Tourism and Events NT domestic and international activities. We promote the Northern Territory as unique holiday destination through cooperative marketing activity with wholesalers, online travel agents and airlines to capitalise on the high domestic awareness of the NT, packaging NT itineraries and experiences and creating a sense of urgency by encouraging consumers to ‘book now’ with enticing deals.

title: Solar for Multi Dwellings Grant Scheme
description: The Solar for Multi Dwellings Grant Scheme provides funding to eligible unit management corporations in the Northern Territory (NT). You can get up to $7,500 (excluding GST) per dwelling to cover up to 50% of the total installation costs for: For example, the maximum grant available for 10 dwellings would be $75,000 to reimburse 50% of at least $150,000.

title: Territory Growth Initiative
description: The Territory Growth Initiative (TGI) provides access to financial support for eligible high-growth potential businesses, to help establish or expand operations and for projects that may not otherwise be able to readily access capital in the Northern Territory. The initiative is responsible for administering: The Collaborative Investment Program supports businesses and projects:

title: Territory Innovation Challenges Program
description: If you’re a business or research or training organisation and want to join other organisations to help solve a challenge set by the Northern Territory (NT) Government, you can apply for the Territory Innovation Challenges Program. If the solution you put forward is picked as the winning entry, you get the chance to negotiate a contract to procure the solution with the government. If the government procures your solution, it will also support you to sell your solution to other buyers in local and export markets.

description: This page has information about gambling amelioration grants in the Northern Territory (NT). The community benefit fund (CBF) receives funds from a levy on electronic gaming machines in licensed hotels and casinos in the NT. The CBF Gambling Amelioration Grants Program (the Program) provides funding to support the promotion of community awareness and education in respect of gambling harms.

description: The gambling research grant program supports research that will help prevent and reduce gambling harm in the NT. The grant program is funded by the community benefit fund (CBF) through a levy on gaming machines in licenced hotels and casinos. To be eligible, your research must be gambling-related. It must also have direct benefit and application to the NT.

description: A global shortage of vehicles has affected NT Fleet’s turnover. This means longer wait times for gifted vehicles to become available. In some cases, applicants may have to wait up to a year from the date they're notified of being successful. This section has information about the vehicle gift program.

title: Crown land grants for community groups
description: Appropriate Crown land is available for eligible community groups to develop community infrastructure at reduced or no rental cost. Community groups can lease community infrastructure through a public and competitive process, or through a direct application. A direct application must clearly address the matters set out in the Release of Crown Land Policy PDF (589.0 KB)

description: Funding is available to increase passenger transport access in remote areas. This is offered under the government's Remote Passenger Transport Program. Proposed services should fill gaps that exist because passenger transport in an area may not be commercially viable or has not been provided for other reasons.

title: Grants for gender equality
description: If you have a project that aims to increase gender equality in the Northern Territory (NT), you can apply for a general grant of up to $5,000. The Office of Gender Equity and Diversity general grants program offers 2 funding rounds each year. Round one is open from July to December and round two is open from January to May.

title: Grants for Seniors Month events

description: Community organisations and businesses can apply for funding to deliver events and activities during Seniors Month. Your business must be registered and hold a specific Seniors Month event. Community organisations or businesses that have previously received Seniors Month grants must have satisfactorily used those grants to apply for another.

title: Multicultural grants
description: The Harmony Grants Program provides funding for initiatives that celebrate and share the Northern Territory’s (NT) diverse cultures. Harmony Week is an annual celebration of Australia’s cultural diversity and the benefits it brings to the Australian community. It occurs each year during the week (Monday to Sunday) that includes 21 March, the United Nations International Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination.

description: If you have a project to support men’s places, you can apply for a grant of up to $20,000 per applicant. You can use the grant to support a range of activities such as: To find out more about the policy and program, go to the Department of People, Sport and Culture website.

description: The Northern Territory (NT) Recreational Fishing Grants Scheme (NTRFGS) recognises the social and economic value of recreational fishing. The scheme offers grant funding for community projects that support and grow recreational fishing in the Territory. Small grant applications are opening soon.

title: Grants for young people
description: The Youth Engagement Grants Program (YEGP) offers grants to community groups and organisations for events and projects that promote positive and meaningful pathways for Territorians aged 12 to 25. The Regional Youth Activities Grants (RYAG) includes delivery and access to after-hours core youth services and programs, including school holiday activities, ensuring that young people can access diverse, engaging and supportive activities. The Northern Territory Government’s investment in these grants and partnerships with young people, youth sector representatives, community groups and organisations will strengthen community ties and promote economic growth.

title: Road Safety NT
description: The Northern Territory Government urges all Territorians to prioritise road safety and drive like our lives are worth living. Road safety is everyone’s responsibility. As individuals and as a community, we have the power to make our roads safer. As part of ongoing efforts to improve road safety outcomes, the Northern Territory Government is encouraging community participation through the Road Safety Grants program. The program provides an opportunity for grass roots organisations, groups and schools to develop and implement road safety initiatives important to their community.

title: Domestic, family and sexual violence prevention
description: Domestic, family and sexual violence (DFSV) is prevalent in the Northern Territory (NT). Our Watch is Australia’s independent organisation to prevent violence against women and their children. They aim to raise awareness about this issue and promote gender equality as a means of preventing violence. Our Watch carries out research, education and policy initiatives, and works in collaboration with governments, organisations and communities to drive change.

title: Back-to-School payment scheme
description: The Back-to-School payment scheme is an NT Government initiative to help parents pay for certain school-related costs. This supports student learning by removing potential barriers for students to engage in school. If eligible, you are entitled to a one-off payment of $200, to be spent by the end of Term 3 in each school year.

description: Students who have resettled in Australia as a humanitarian entrant or refugee may apply for the Charles See Kee Scholarship. The scholarship aims to help students to further their employment and general life outcomes. Applications are managed by the Charles Darwin University (CDU). They're usually open from November to early March.

title: Higher education scholarships
description: The higher education scholarship helps Northern Territory (NT) school graduates undertake tertiary studies that lead to employment in the NT. Up to 20 scholarships are offered each year to eligible year 12 graduates. The scholarships provide $5,000 a year to study their first undergraduate degree at Charles Darwin University (CDU).

title: Inclusive Education Support Grant
description: If you’re an eligible organisation working with children with disability, you can apply for funding for services or programs that improve their education. The Inclusive Education Support Grant (IESG) offers funding up to $100,000 per service or program on an annual basis for 3 years - from January 2023 until the end of 2025. If you have different programs and services, you can submit multiple applications. Read the IESG information guide for applicants and recipients DOCX (694.9 KB).

title: Get Learn to Swim vouchers for your child
description: If your child is under 5 years old and not enrolled in school, you can apply for the Learn to Swim program in the Northern Territory (NT). The program gives your child two $100 Learn to Swim vouchers each year - one in January and one in July. You can use the vouchers to enrol them in swimming lessons with a registered activity provider.

title: Grants for gender equality
description: Northern Territory (NT) students at Charles Darwin University (CDU) can apply for a Minister for Equality scholarship. The scholarship can help with the cost of their vocational education and training (VET) or higher education. Below are the VET scholarships available.

Powering the Regions Industrial Transformation Stream
The Industrial Transformation Stream supports the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions at existing industrial facilities in regional Australia.
How to apply for funding
1
Step 1 - Read the program guidelines
2
Step 2: Observe the key dates
3
Step 3: Prepare your response
Background

The $400 million Industrial Transformation Stream (ITS) is open for applications supporting a range of industrial decarbonisation solutions, including but not limited, to electrification, energy efficiency, low emissions processing and fuel switching. Enabling technologies such as energy storage, demand management and critical infrastructure supporting onsite decarbonisation may also be considered. Funding is guided by the Program Focus Areas.

About the program

The objectives of the ITS Program are to:

Support industrial facilities in regional Australia to reduce emissions in line with Australia’s 2030 emissions reduction goals and transition to net zero by 2050.
Improve technology readiness and commercial readiness of renewable energy technologies, energy efficiency technologies and electrification technologies that will result in a significant reduction in scope 1 and/or scope 2 emissions or improve the energy efficiency of an industrial process or processes.
Encourage technology demonstration, collaboration and knowledge sharing across industry and/or regions.
Support upskilling of existing industrial workforce in new decarbonisation activities.
How it works

The ITS Program is ongoing and will accept applications that contribute to the Program objectives and align with active ITS Focus Areas. The assessment process involves up to two application stages:

Expressions of interest; and
Full application.

Battery Breakthrough Initiative
In the 2024-2025 Federal Budget, the Australian Government announced the Battery Breakthrough Initiative with $500 million of funding to promote the development of battery manufacturing capabilities in Australia.
Battery Breakthrough Initiative
1
Step 1 - Read the program guidelines
2
Step 2 - Prepare your response 
Background

The Battery Breakthrough Initiative (BBI) was announced as part of the Future Made in Australia (FMA) policy agenda of the Australian Government. Central to the National Battery Strategy, the program has been designed in consultation with the Department of Industry, Science and Resources (DISR) and a broad range of stakeholders.

For more information about ARENA’s consultation process, read the Consultation Summary Paper (PDF 234KB).

How it works

Funding provided through BBI may include capital grants, production incentives or other payments deemed appropriate by ARENA for the project.

The program’s objective is to fund projects that contribute to one or more of the following program outcomes:

Enhance Australia’s battery manufacturing capability to improve supply chain resilience and support emissions reduction
Commercialise battery manufacturing processes and technologies that can contribute to emissions reduction.

Applicants should review the key documents below before submitting an application for the program:

BBI Program Guidelines (PDF 530KB)
BBI Focus Areas (PDF 278KB)
BBI Frequently Asked Questions (PDF 290KB)
Public information webinars

ARENA will run two virtual information sessions, which will discuss the Program Guidelines and funding eligibility. Both webinars will cover the same information. Register with the links below:

Wednesday 17 September 2025, 3:00PM – 4:00PM AEST

Hydrogen Headstart Round 2
The Hydrogen Headstart program (the Program) provides long-term revenue support to underwrite large-scale renewable hydrogen production projects.

Hydrogen Headstart Round 2
1
Step 1: Program Overview
2
Step 2: How to apply
3
Step 3: Observe the key dates
Background

In the May 2024 Federal Budget, the Australian Government announced $2 billion of funding for Round 2 of the Hydrogen Headstart program. The Program is designed to fund large-scale hydrogen production projects to accelerate development of Australia’s renewable hydrogen industry, catalyse clean energy industries and help Australia connect to new global hydrogen supply chains.

Round 2 of the Program will be delivered by ARENA. Hydrogen Headstart Round 2 follows the completion of Hydrogen Headstart Round 1, with further detail on successful funding recipients available here.

About the program

The Objectives of Hydrogen Headstart Round 2 are to:

Produce renewable hydrogen at scale in Australia, facilitating an accelerated pathway to the technical and commercial viability of renewable hydrogen production and use, in alignment with the priority use cases and goals of the National Hydrogen Strategy;
Support domestic decarbonisation, build industry capability and provide for new economic opportunities in our manufacturing and export industries;
Provide price discovery and transparency in relation to the current and projected economics for renewable hydrogen (and derivative product) technologies;
Reduce barriers for future deployments through developing construction expertise, skilled labour, intellectual property, developing supply chains and attracting key equipment manufacturers and private sector capital (debt, equity and offtake); and
Facilitate knowledge sharing throughout industry to assist with maturing the Australian r

Future Made in Australia Innovation Fund
In the May 2024 Federal Budget, the Australian Government announced investment in the Future Made in Australia Innovation Fund (the Program). The Program, administered by ARENA will provide up to $1.5 billion in grant funding, focusing on renewable energy and low emission technologies critical to Australia’s renewable energy transition.
Future Made in Australia Innovation Fund

About the program

The Future Made in Australia (FMA) Innovation Fund (the Program) administered by ARENA will provide up to $1.5 billion in grant funding to support pre-commercial innovation, demonstration and deployment of renewable energy and low emission technologies (Technologies), to support Australia’s transition to a net zero economy. FMA is an ongoing program which will remain open until funding is exhausted or ARENA closes the Program. Successful projects are expected to achieve one or more of the following Program Outcomes:

increase uptake of the Technologies through innovation and commercialisation activities.
improve Australia’s capability and capacity to produce or manufacture the Technologies.
support and/or contribute to stronger supply chains to enable increased use of the Technologies.
improve knowledge, skills and/or workforce participation associated with the Technologies.

ARENA undertook targeted consultation through March and April 2025 with market participants, key consultancies and industry bodies and associations, to understand and identify industries and areas most suited for funding within the priority areas.

The FMA Innovation Fund launched in December 2025 and will be an ongoing, open program. Grant funding will be available across three Priorities (the Priorities):

$750 million to Green Metals including iron, steel, alumina and aluminium.

Solar Sunshot
On 28 March 2024, the Federal Government announced it will invest $1 billion in the new Solar Sunshot Program (the program). The program seeks to support the commercialisation of Australian solar photovoltaic (PV) innovations and to enhance solar PV supply chains in Australia.
About the program

The Solar SunShot Program aims to support innovative solar PV manufacturing facilities in Australia across the solar supply chain.

The total funding allocated for the Program is up to $1 billion (Total Funding Allocation) and is delivered through funding rounds. Funding Announcements detail the amount of funding allocated under a funding round, the specific types of projects we are seeking to fund, and the process we will use to fund projects.

Information on current and past funding rounds can be found below, and Funding Announcements should be read in conjunction with:

Solar Sunshot – Program Guidelines (PDF 257KB)

All Program dates / times are Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST).

Ultra Low-Cost Solar PV R&D Funding Round
 
How to Apply for Funding
1
Step 1 – Read Funding Round Announcement & Program Guidelines
2

3
Step 3 – Prepare your response
Background

ARENA is continuing its support for research and development (R&D) and commercialisation of Australian solar PV, with a new $60 million funding round to push innovation and take solar to ultra low-cost levels. ARENA’s ULCS goal is to achieve a Levelised Cost of Electricity (LCOE) for utility-scale solar PV of under $20 per MWh.

How it works

We are seeking proposals from a broad range of Applicants including, but not limited to, Australian universities and research groups and innovative businesses to accelerate cost reductions and meet ARENA’s ULCS LCOE goal.

This is a competitive funding round under our Research, Development and Commercialisation (RDC) Program (formerly referred to as TRAC Program). It is open to applications for projects which have achieved a Technology Readiness Level of at least three at the time of applying.

Information webinars

ARENA held two webinars to introduce the Ultra Low-Cost Solar PV R&D Funding Round on 17 and 29 July, with the same content discussed at each webinar. The recording is below, as well as slides.


View slides (PDF 1.5MB)
Round Objectives

We seek ambitious Proposals that can make a significant contribution through six Focus Areas across two streams:

Stream 1: Cells and Modules
Increase efficiency: Develop large size cells for next generation solar modules at over 30% module efficiency.
Reduce cost: Reduce system cost to below 30 cents per watt.
Improve stability: Improve stability of cells and modules for utility-scale solar farms to meet a minimum asset life of 30 years, with longer asset operating lifetimes expected.

The Program is closed to new Expression of Interest applications as of 11:59 PM AEDT 19 December 2025

Background

The Regional Microgrids Program (the Program) seeks to support the development and deployment of renewable energy microgrids across regional Australia that contribute to the Program Outcomes.

ARENA has allocated funding across two Streams under the Program, and each Stream has its own Outcomes.

Regional Australia Microgrid Pilots (Stream A)

The aim of Stream A is to fund Projects that contribute to the following Outcomes:

Innovation and/or acceleration of the development and deployment of equipment and technology solutions that enable the coordinated use of distributed renewable energy technologies.
Improved resilience and reliability of electricity supply in regional areas.
Resolving one or more of the remaining barriers to deployment of microgrid solutions.

 

First Nations Community Microgrids (Stream B)

The aim of Stream B is to fund Projects that contribute to the following Outcomes:

Reduced cost of energy and other community benefits in First Nations Communities.
Cleaner and more reliable energy in First Nations Communities.
First Nations Communities are empowered to participate in their electricity supply arrangements and the development of energy infrastructure.
Resolving one or more of the remaining barriers to deployment of microgrid solutions.

Background

The October 2022 Federal Budget provided $200 million for the Community Batteries for Household Solar budget measure to deploy 400 community batteries across Australia. Of this, $171 million was allocated to ARENA to deliver at least 342 batteries. The Community Batteries Funding Initiative under the Advancing Renewables Program (ARP) seeks to support the deployment of community batteries across Australia to lower energy bills, cut emissions, reduce pressure on the electricity grid and enable further distributed solar installations.

Through Round 1 of the Community Batteries Funding Initiative, ARENA approved $124.7 million to deploy 318 batteries in all states and the Northern Territory. ARENA has allocated the remaining $46.3 million of funding towards Community Batteries Round 2 (Round 2).

How it works

The Round 2 Funding Announcement details the process ARENA will use to fund projects. Round 2 will be delivered as a competitive process under the ARP as defined in the ARP Guidelines. The Funding Announcement should be read in conjunction with the ARP Guidelines below (which apply to Round 2).

National Industrial Transformation Program
The $40 million National Industrial Transformation Program (NIT Program) will support the reduction of scope 1 and scope 2 emissions at existing or planned industrial facilities across Australia.

This opportunity is closed to new applications.
How to apply for funding
1
Step 1 - Read the program guidelines
2
Step 2: Observe the key dates
3
Step 3: Prepare your response
Background

Industrial transformation at ARENA is intended to support the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions related to industrial activity. A range of technology solutions targeting industrial emissions abatement may be supported, from electrification and energy efficiency to fuel switching and zero emissions vehicles. Enabling technologies such as energy storage, demand management and critical infrastructure supporting onsite decarbonisation will also be considered. Projects can range from studies to scale demonstration and deployment.

There are two programs relevant to industrial transformation:

The $400 million Industrial Transformation Stream (which is a stream under the $1.9 billion Powering the Regions Fund) and
The $40 million National Industrial Transformation Program.

See the guide below for further explanation on the two programs:
Which Industrial Decarbonisation Program Is Right For Me (PDF 128 KB)

About the program 

The objectives of the NIT Program are to: 

Support industrial facilities in Australia to reduce emissions in line with Australia’s 2030 emissions reduction goals and transition to net zero by 2050. 
Improve technology readiness and commercial readiness of renewable energy technologies, energy efficiency technologies and electrification technologies that will result in a significant reduction in scope 1 and/or scope 2 emissions or improve the energy effici

This opportunity is closed to new applications. CHASING MILLIONS Invisible Republic Pty Ltd Genre Action adventure, Comedy, Thriller, Crime, Mystery Director Stephen Burke Writers Katherine Thomson, Stephen Burke Producers Jane Doolan, Michael Wrenn Distributor Bonsai Films International Sales Level K Synopsis Belfast 2004. Northern Ireland has been at peace for six years, but old enmities, and mistrust remain. Chasing Millions tells the story of the biggest bank heist in Irish, British (and Australian) history making reluctant partners of ambitious Australian police officer, Diana, with gruff, veteran Northern Irish detective, Crawford, as they investigate and seek to solve the crime while navigating their way through the minefield of a fragile peace. description: BALLPARK NORMAL 8 x 30 mins Drama Queen Pty Ltd Genre Comedy, Drama Writer/Director Sofya Gollan Producers Justine Flynn, Naomi Just Synopsis A coming of age drama-comedy about rebelling against being labelled disabled AF. But with a secret past and a city full of enemies, is she now the hunter or the hunted? DARK MODE 6 x 60 mins Wooden Horse Genre Drama, Thriller Creator/Writer Anna Barnes Executive Producers Jude Troy, Richard Finlayson Synopsis Adapted from the novel of the same name by Ashley Kalagian Blunt, Dark Mode is a tech-thriller that follows Dr Reagan Walton, a GP whose life is turned upside down by a stalker. In an attempt to regain control, she erases all traces of her digital presence – going into ‘dark mode.’ But when a serial killer with an online following resurfaces in Sydney, Reagan is forced out of hiding. Dark Mode is a fast-paced thriller for our times, exploring the complexities of online safety and the digital ages' impact on privacy and personal relationships.

title: Guidelines Final Certification
amount_text: Final certification - Guidelines - Producer Offset - Funding and Support - Screen Australia Close Subscribe Contact Us Site Search Menu Close HomeScreen Australia Home Funding and Support Data and Insights View by Screen Australia Home Home Search The Screen Guide Screen News Your free industry news source Articles Intel Video Podcast Notices Guides Upcoming productions See what's in production and upcoming All upcoming productions Documentary productions Drama productions The Screen Guide Searc
description: A final certificate entitles a company to receive the Producer Offset. Once a project is completed, and audited, you must submit an application along with all required attachments (including a copy of the completed project and a specified statutory declaration) and in accordance with the instructions on the form. The copy of the project is important for the assessment process, and is covered by the tax secrecy provisions. Applicants should feel free to watermark the film if they wish. You will also need to lodge expenditure statements attesting to the claimed QAPE of the project. These will include the general ledger, final cost report and a QAPE spreadsheet. A registered company auditor must audit your QAPE claim in accordance with the Producer Offset Rules 2018.

title: Guidelines Provisional Certification
amount_text: Provisional certification - Guidelines - Producer Offset - Funding and Support - Screen Australia Close Subscribe Contact Us Site Search Menu Close HomeScreen Australia Home Funding and Support Data and Insights View by Screen Australia Home Home Search The Screen Guide Screen News Your free industry news source Articles Intel Video Podcast Notices Guides Upcoming productions See what's in production and upcoming All upcoming productions Documentary productions Drama productions The Screen Guide
description: A provisional certificate states that, based on the information presented in the application, we consider that the proposed project would meet the eligibility requirements for the Producer Offset, if it is made in accordance with the information provided. A provisional certificate does not guarantee that a project will qualify for the Producer Offset. An application must be made for a final certificate (and a final certificate must be issued) once the project is completed in order to claim the Producer Offset. If a provisional certificate is not issued for a project this does not prevent you applying for a final certificate in relation to that project. You may apply for a provisional certificate at any time during development, financing or production. To apply for a provisional certificate, an applicant company must complete an application form and provide it, and all attachments, to the POCU. Incomplete applications will not be assessed.

description: SOLD! WHO BROKE THE AUSTRALIAN DREAM? 1 x 60 mins Wedge-Tail Pictures Director Bill Code Writers Bill Code, Mark Humphries, Craig Reucassel, Evan Williams Producers Bill Code, Justine Moyle Executive Producers Adam Farrington-Williams, Craig Reucassel Broadcaster BINGE, FOXTEL Synopsis Mark Humphries is sick of standing in lines for rentals. But if a C grade celebrity can’t afford to buy a house, then how do normal people do it. Mark dives headfirst into Australia’s complex housing debate, looking at the current house prices, soaring rent, and the "Australian Dream" that feels more out of reach than ever. JUDGMENT: CASES THAT CHANGED AUSTRALIA 4 x 48 mins Wildbear Treasure Pty Ltd Producer Alan Erson Writers Larissa Behrendt, Victoria Midwinter Pitt Directors Larissa Behrendt, Victoria Midwinter Pitt Executive Producer Michael Tear Broadcaster ABC International Sales ABC Commercial Synopsis Judgment: Cases that Changed Australia explores pivotal High Court cases through those with the greatest stakes in the Court’s decisions, lawyers and their arguments, and the consequences for Australia. ROBODEBT (WORKING TITLE) 3 x 60 mins CJZ Pty Ltd Director Ben Lawrence Writer Jane Allen Executive Producers Paula Bycroft, Michael Cordell Broadcaster SBS Synopsis A series that combines documentary storytelling with drama to uncover the human story of the notorious “Robodebt" scandal that struck at the heart of inequality and social cohesion in Australia. A brave bunch of Australians took a stand against Centrelink debts that were unfair, inaccurate and illegal. This is the story of their heroic fight against impossible odds. END GAME 3 x 48 mins Smashing Films Executive Producers Daniel Brown, Steve Bibb, Dean Gibson Broadcaster ABC International Sales ABC Commercial Synopsis Fed up with excuses, Tony Armstrong is on a global mission to find solutions to combat the rising tide of racism in Australian sports to create real change for future generations.

description: THE HUNT FOR THE LAST NAZIS 80 mins HIDDEN IN VIEW PTY LTD Genre Documentary Director Dan Goldberg Writers Dan Goldberg, David Roach Producers Danny Ben-Moshe, Dan Goldberg Executive Producers Craig Graham Broadcaster SBS International Sales Abacus Media Rights Synopsis With exclusive access to never-seen-before classified government files and explosive video and audio recordings, this is the scandalous story of how thousands of alleged Nazi war criminals hid in Australia after WWII and managed to evade justice – despite a government-sanctioned Special Investigations Unit desperate to prosecute them. TESTIMONY 84 mins Closer Films Pty Ltd Genre Environment & Social Issues Director Maya Newell Writers Paul Kabai, Pariala Sedrick Waia, Pabai Pabai, Dr Togiab McRose Elu Producers Maya Newell, Larissa Behrendt, Sophie Hyde, Alex Kelly, Lisa Sherrard Executive Producers Dr Togiab McRose Elu, Deanne Weir Synopsis As the climate emergency rises before them, Torres Strait Islanders too rise up, suing the government for failing to prevent climate harm. While the court can only hold part of the story, we dance, remember, and dream of possible futures with Saibai and her people. This film is their testimony. UNTITLED WILD PACIFIC MEDIA PRODUCTION 3 x 52 mins CROC KINGDOM SPV PTY LTD Genre Natural History Director Nick Robinson Writers Mark Coles Smith, Nick Robinson, Peta Ayers Producers Nick Robinson, Peta Ayers Executive Producers Electra Manikakis Synopsis Over one dramatic year on a wild Australian river, the secret lives of saltwater crocodiles are revealed - from fierce rivalries to unexpected alliances - as they navigate a rich ecosystem teeming with wallabies, dingoes, sea eagles, and more.

amount_text: Qualifying expenditure - Guidelines - Producer Offset - Funding and Support - Screen Australia Close Subscribe Contact Us Site Search Menu Close HomeScreen Australia Home Funding and Support Data and Insights View by Screen Australia Home Home Search The Screen Guide Screen News Your free industry news source Articles Intel Video Podcast Notices Guides Upcoming productions See what's in production and upcoming All upcoming productions Documentary productions Drama productions The Screen Guide Se
description: There are two general expenditure concepts relevant to the Producer Offset, which are outlined below: A project’s ‘production expenditure’ is defined in section 376-125(1) of the Income Tax Assessment Act 1997 (ITAA) as the expenditure incurred by the applicant company in, or that is reasonably attributable to, the ‘making of the film’ (ie the project). The making of the film means doing the things necessary for the production of the first copy of the project. This includes development, pre-production, principal photography and post-production activities and any other activities that are necessary to bring a production up to the state where it is ready to be distributed, broadcast or exhibited to the general public.

description: EMILY: I AM KAM 1 x 58 mins Emily SPV Pty Ltd, Tamarind Tree Pictures Writer/Director Danielle MacLean Producers Anna Grieve, Danielle MacLean Broadcaster NITV Synopsis Celebrating the legacy of Australia’s most significant female artist - Emily Kam Kngwarray. We travel with her descendants as they revive awely ceremonies and collaborate on a major retrospective exhibition, reaffirming her connection to Country. OUR MEDICINE S2 8 x 30 mins PiNK PEPPER Pty Ltd and Periscope Pictures Pty Ltd Producers Taryne Laffar, Sam Field Directors Karla Hart, Kimberley Benjamin Broadcaster NITV Synopsis In Series 2, new and returning First Nations healers, doctors, nurses, paramedics and medical professionals push themselves to their limits, trying to achieve better health outcomes for their First Nations patients and communities. THE SERVANT Willow Films Pty Ltd Genre Horror Producers David Jowsey, Ivan Sen Director Ivan Sen Distributor Dark Matter Distribution Synopsis An Indigenous student arrives at a remote homestead to record its history, but as the days pass, haunted by unease and contradictions, she begins to question her host, the house—and her own grip on reality. The deeper she digs, the more the past begins to consume her. SONGMAN Djali House Pty Ltd Genre Drama Director Stephen Page Writers Stephen Page, Alana Valentine Producers Hunter Page-Lochard, Huna Amweero, Belinda Mravicic Distributor Maslow Entertainment International Sales Level K Synopsis A flamboyant First Nations composer and former child star, suspended in purgatory, creates one final song before passing on, revisiting a life spent performing in drag bars and composing for Australia’s leading Indigenous dance company, finding his true voice when his Yugambeh father gifts him language and sacred knowledge.

description: There are tax concessions to support national conservation efforts. As of 1 January 2024, the Register of Environmental Organisations no longer exists as a register. The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) now handle environmental organisations’ Deductible Gift Recipient (DGR) endorsement or status. If you were endorsed as a public fund on the register before this change, transitional provisions apply.

title: Spreading your environmental or heritage donation over multiple years
description: If you donate to an eligible environmental or National Trust organisation, or enter an eligible conservation covenant, you can elect to spread (apportion) the donation/covenant deductions against your tax return over up to 5 income years. For more information refer to subdivision 30‑DB of the Income Tax Assessment Act 1997. Complete the relevant apportionment election form to elect to spread your donation or covenant concession over several years, or to vary a previous election.

description: Under the Government Procurement (Judicial Review) Act 2018 (Cth), certain types of procurements may be suspended while complaints are being investigated or injunctions are being considered. We may issue a Public Interest Certificate (PIC) in circumstances where we consider that it is not in the public interest for a procurement process to be suspended. See Handling complaints under the Government Procurement (Judicial Review) Act 2018 on the Department of Finance website for further information on PICs.
